objective: Move the positive VPI C++ Remap operator result one step closer to the accepted MMAPI/VPI/NVENC device-stage path by replacing PerspectiveWarp with Remap on the pitch-linear scratch stage in a minimal diagnostic sample.

entry_context.previous_result: VPI C++ Remap works on CPU/CUDA, CUDA beats OpenCV CPU on tested identity/wave maps, and NV12_ER CPU/CUDA works in the standalone probe.
entry_context.accepted_device_path: block-linear NV12 main chain -> NvBufSurfTransform to pitch-linear NV12_ER scratch -> VPI CUDA PerspectiveWarp -> NvBufSurfTransform back -> NVENC
entry_context.current_question: Can Remap replace PerspectiveWarp at the VPI scratch stage without green output, tearing, unreadable output, or broad MMAPI rewrite?
entry_context.quality_boundary: resid_r15_s07 remains frozen; this is an operator/device-stage diagnostic, not EIS quality tuning.

result.decision: Remap can replace PerspectiveWarp in a minimal MMAPI/VPI/NVENC diagnostic sample when dimensions satisfy WarpGrid/output-size requirements. The original wave black-border artifact was caused by zero-border sampling outside the source and was fixed with wave_safe FOV-safe scaling.

dimension_boundary.regular05_640x360: failed because WarpGrid aligned height to 368 and Remap requires output size to match the warp map
dimension_boundary.diagnostic_640x368_padded: identity, wave, and wave_safe ran with rc=0

result.claim_boundary: Diagnostic operator/device-stage integration only. No Regular EIS quality, no mesh/local-warp quality, no zero-copy, no full-pipeline acceleration.

frozen_variables: same MMAPI decode/encode structure where possible
pitch-linear NV12_ER scratch remains the first target
identity remap first, then local wave diagnostic map
